mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates indicate an accommodative monetary policy environment, reducing the cost of capital for businesses and encouraging investment, which translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within 0.1%) in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If, following a trigger event, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand. This synergistic strength suggests a healthy economic expansion, driving corporate earnings growth and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average > 1200k
failure_condition: If, when both RSAFS and HOUST conditions are met, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of occurrences over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of stable or improving industrial production, the supply side of the economy is functioning efficiently, supporting corporate profitability. This efficiency, especially in a low inflation environment, leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO is rising and CPIAUCSL is below 3% over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly accelerating consumer price inflation above a critical threshold forces the central bank to adopt a more hawkish stance, leading to higher interest rates and a contraction in economic activity, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, after the trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-stat < -1.5) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a robust and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending. This sustained economic strength provides a positive backdrop for corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant slowdown in GDP growth combined with persistent high inflation indicates stagflationary pressures. This environment erodes corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth < 1% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth > 3%
failure_condition: If, when both GDP growth is low and inflation is high, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening (rising FEDFUNDS) combined with a tightening labor market (low UNRATE), the risk of an economic hard landing increases. This scenario typically leads to a re-pricing of equities downwards and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, under the specified conditions, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of observations, or if the average return is not significantly negative (p-value > 0.1) over a 5-year period.
